name: Open-Ended Waveguide
description: An open-ended waveguide antenna formed by terminating a rectangular or circular waveguide with an open aperture. It radiates electromagnetic energy directly from the waveguide opening, creating a directional beam with moderate gain. The radiation characteristics depend on the waveguide dimensions and the field distribution at the aperture.

aperture_efficiency.name: Aperture Efficiency
aperture_efficiency.description: Efficiency of power radiation from the aperture

flange_size.name: Flange Size
flange_size.description: Size of conducting flange around aperture

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting appropriate waveguide dimensions for single-mode operation, calculating aperture field distribution, and optimizing radiation characteristics through proper termination and flange design.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency and Mode
steps.description: Select operating frequency and ensure TE10 mode propagation in rectangular waveguide
steps.formula: f_c = c / (2 * a) for TE10 cutoff frequency

steps.title: Calculate Waveguide Dimensions
steps.description: Size waveguide for single-mode operation with proper impedance matching
steps.formula: a = lambda_g / 2, b = a / 2, where lambda_g = lambda_0 / sqrt(1 - (f_c/f)^2)

steps.title: Determine Aperture Field Distribution
steps.description: Calculate electric and magnetic field distributions at the aperture opening
steps.formula: E_y = E_0 * cos(pi * x / a) for TE10 mode

steps.title: Calculate Radiation Pattern
steps.description: Compute far-field radiation pattern using aperture field distribution
steps.formula: F(theta, phi) = integral of aperture fields with phase factor exp(j*k*r'*sin(theta))

steps.title: Optimize Flange Design
steps.description: Design conducting flange to minimize diffraction and improve pattern
steps.formula: D_flange >= 3 * lambda_0 for effective ground plane

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine input impedance including radiation resistance and reactance
steps.formula: Z_in = R_rad + j*X_rad, where R_rad = 377 * G_rad

equations.resonantFrequency: f_c = c / (2 * a) for TE10 cutoff
equations.inputImpedance: Z_in = (377 * b * lambda_g) / (a * lambda_0) * (1 + j * tan(beta * l))
equations.gain: G = (4 * pi * A_eff) / lambda_0^2 = (4 * pi * eta_ap * a * b) / lambda_0^2
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta, phi) = (j * k * E_0 * a * b) / (2 * pi * r) * sinc(k*a*sin(theta)*cos(phi)/2) * sinc(k*b*sin(theta)*sin(phi)/2)
equations.bandwidth: BW = 2 * (f - f_c) / f for single-mode operation

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with radiation loading at aperture, include reactive component from aperture susceptance
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply Huygens principle with aperture field distribution, use Fourier transform for far-field calculation
keyAssumptions: Infinite ground plane approximation for flange

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 12
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 9
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 15
